1.Title: Alternating Response Time Spikes
1.Insight: For Homepage, noticeable **response time spikes** (2400ms, 2430ms) coincided with fluctuating [Requests Per Second](#ClientMetrics) even as [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) remained constant, suggesting intermittent backend bottlenecks under steady load.
1.Details: [Response Time](#ClientMetrics) alternated between lows (~1840ms) and highs (**2430ms**) at 9:20 PM UTC. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) showed peaks (6.4 RPS at 9:19 PM UTC) and significant drops (2.0 RPS at 9:20 PM UTC). [Virtual Users](#ClientMetrics) were constant at 10 until 9:20 PM UTC, then decreased to 7 at 9:20 PM UTC.
1.Recommendation: Consider monitoring server-side metrics (CPU, memory, DB latency) during these intervals and review application logs for resource contention or thread starvation. Increase backend observability to pinpoint intermittent delays.

2.Title: Sharp Throughput Decline Near End
2.Insight: A **sharp drop** in [Requests Per Second](#ClientMetrics) (from 5.6 RPS to 2.0 RPS at 9:20 PM UTC) for Homepage coincided with a reduction in [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics), indicating possible client-side throttling or early session termination.
2.Details: [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) decreased sharply to **2.0 RPS** at 9:20 PM UTC as [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) simultaneously dropped from 10 to 7 at 9:20 PM UTC. [Response Time](#ClientMetrics) briefly fell to **1840ms** at 9:20 PM UTC.
2.Recommendation: Analyze load test engine logs for reasons behind virtual user drop (e.g., errors, timeouts, or resource constraints). Review session cleanup logic and test configuration for premature termination triggers.

3.Title: Inverse Response Time-Throughput Pattern
3.Insight: Periods where [Response Time](#ClientMetrics) decreased for Homepage corresponded to **increased throughput**, suggesting backend improvements or temporary cache effectiveness.
3.Details: [Response Time](#ClientMetrics) declined from 3240ms at 9:18 PM UTC to 1980ms at 9:19 PM UTC, while [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) rose from 3.6 RPS at 9:18 PM UTC to **6.4 RPS** at 9:19 PM UTC, maintaining >5 RPS through 9:19 PM UTC. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) were stable at 10 throughout this period.
3.Recommendation: Review backend performance optimizations or caching strategies applied during the test window. Validate if temporary improvements are sustainable under continuous high load.

4.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) completed successfully with minor latency fluctuations. Client-side symptoms included alternating response time spikes and throughput drops, but no errors or server-side bottlenecks were observed.

1.Title: RPS Drop Despite Steady Users
1.Insight: While virtual users for Homepage remained constant at **10**, [response time](#ClientMetrics) fluctuated significantly and [requests per second](#ClientMetrics) dropped to **2.0 RPS** by 9:20 PM UTC. This correlated slowdown suggests the server experienced increasing latency, likely due to resource contention or backend limitations.
1.Details: [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) were stable at **10** from 9:18 PM UTC to 9:20 PM UTC, dropping to **7** at 9:20 PM UTC. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) started at 3.6 RPS at 9:18 PM UTC, peaked at 6.4 RPS by 9:19 PM UTC, and declined to 2.0 RPS at 9:20 PM UTC despite stable user counts. [Response time](#ClientMetrics) fluctuated between 1840ms and 2430ms from 9:19 PM UTC to 9:20 PM UTC, with a trough at **1840ms** at 9:20 PM UTC.
1.Recommendation: Consider investigating server-side bottlenecks—such as database, network, or compute resource limitations. Profile application performance at higher RPS levels and review backend scaling configurations.

2.Title: Response Time Fluctuation Under Load
2.Insight: Observed substantial [response time](#ClientMetrics) variability for Homepage with peaks up to **2430ms** and troughs as low as **1840ms**, while virtual users remained stable. This suggests inconsistent server responsiveness under load.
2.Details: [Response time](#ClientMetrics) dropped from 3240ms at 9:18 PM UTC to 2090ms at 9:19 PM UTC, then fluctuated between 1840ms and 2430ms from 9:19 PM UTC to 9:20 PM UTC. Notable peaks were observed at 9:19 PM UTC (**2400ms**), 9:20 PM UTC (**2250ms**), and 9:20 PM UTC (**2430ms**). [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) varied but remained between 5.0 and 6.1 RPS during these response time fluctuations.
2.Recommendation: Consider enabling server-side monitoring and logging during load tests to pinpoint bottlenecks. Review application code for inefficient operations that may cause periodic delays under concurrent access.

3.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) completed with performance degradation. Response times fluctuated with multiple peaks, while throughput dropped toward the end, indicating potential backend constraints.
